methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Innoviz Technologies is a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The relevant peer universe consists of five private frontier-tech competitors: Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist within the sector, limiting traditional peer-median approaches.

The Peer-Median method is inapplicable due to an insufficient narrow peer set. While five same-sector peers have been identified, none satisfy the filtering criteria of matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier—each filter yielded fewer than three datapoints. A broad all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and revenue profiles across the Drones & Autonomous space. The absence of public peers and the stage-mismatch among private comparables (most remain well-capitalized but unlisted) prevents reliable triangulation through standard multiples.

Given these constraints, no quantitative valuation range can be derived with defensibility. The analysis instead identifies the core limiting factor: the sector lacks sufficient homogeneous comparables at Innoviz's stage to anchor a triangulated valuation. Any forward analysis would require either (1) expansion to adjacent sectors with structural similarities, (2) access to recent round-level pricing data for the five identified peers, or (3) application of discounted cash flow methods where near-term revenue and margin forecasts exist. Without these inputs, peer-based valuation remains speculative.
